title: haplopappus racemiger
intro: Haplopappus racemiger, also known as the cluster-headed rabbitbrush, is a shrub that belongs to the sunflower family and is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Haplopappus racemiger can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
